title: carya glabra var. villosa
intro: Carya glabra var. villosa, also known as the hairy shagbark hickory, is a variety of hickory tree native to North America, characterized by its hairy young stems and smooth, gray bark with deep furrows on older trees. The medicinal properties of Carya glabra var. villosa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Carya glabra var. villosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
